content.text:
**Single H100 80GB** — for this project specifically.

## Key reasons

**1. FP8 RL is H100-only**
Your GRPO config has `fp8_rl: true`, which requires H100's FP8 E4M3 tensor cores. A100 has no native FP8 — you'd lose the 1.4× speedup and 60% VRAM reduction during RL. This alone is decisive.

**2. Unsloth is single-GPU optimized**
Unsloth doesn't support multi-GPU (DDP/FSDP). Splitting across 2x A100 would mean using standard HF TRL, losing the 2× speed + 70% VRAM advantages that motivated ADR-002.

**3. QLoRA makes VRAM a non-issue**
Your largest model (Qwen3.5-35B-A3B) fits in ~17.5GB at QLoRA 4-bit, well within 80GB. Even BF16 dense models (32B = ~64GB) fit. The extra 80GB from a second A100 buys nothing you need.

**4. Memory bandwidth**
H100 SXM has ~3.35 TB/s vs A100's ~2 TB/s — ~1.67× faster for memory-bound operations like attention and LoRA adapter updates.

**5. vLLM GRPO generation backend**
The GRPO config uses vLLM as the generation backend (for 11× faster RL inference). Running vLLM + training on 2x A100 requires careful multi-process coordination; on a single H100 it's straightforward.

## When 2x A100 would make sense
Only if you needed full BF16 fine-tuning of a 70B+ model with no quantization. None of your models require that.

The project was designed for single H100 from the start — stick with it.

content.text:
The data tells a clear story. Here's my assessment:

## Two real gaps, one non-issue

**1. Behavior distribution is skewed (the main problem)**

| Behavior | Actual | Target | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| cooperative | 41.7% | 60.0% | **−18.3%** |
| adversarial_probing | 30.4% | 15.0% | **+15.4%** |
| digressing | 13.9% | 10.0% | +3.9% |
| invalid_tool_inputs | 14.0% | 15.0% | −1.0% |

Adversarial conversations are 2× overrepresented. Cooperative is your dominant real-traffic pattern (60%) but only 42% of training data. This will bias the model toward over-hedging on normal cooperative flows.

**2. Task B and Task C have zero data** — you'll need those before fine-tuning the specialist and graph extraction models.

**3. Volume is not a problem** — 2,950 is already 3× the spec. More data won't help if the distribution is wrong.

## Recommendation

**Don't generate more yet.** Fix the distribution first by one of two approaches:

- **Cheaper**: Downsample `adversarial_probing` from 897 → ~450 before training. Brings the ratio to roughly 55/20/14/14 — not perfect but much closer, zero cost.
- **Better**: Generate ~1,800 cooperative conversations to hit the 60% target. Since cooperative flows are shorter and simpler, cost per sample is lower than what you've already spent.

Also check whether Task B/C data is needed before your next training run — if you're only fine-tuning the Cat A winner first, Task A is sufficient for now.

165	def _humanise_condition(condition: str) -> str:
166	    """Convert a snake_case condition name to a readable phrase."""
167	    import re
168	    cleaned = condition.replace("proceed_from_", "").replace("branch_", "")
169	    cleaned = cleaned.replace("_", " ")                      # underscores → spaces first
170	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\b[Ss]\d+\b", "", cleaned)           # remove S1, S2 … (now word-bounded)
171	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\bto\b", "", cleaned)                 # remove bare "to" connector
172	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", cleaned).strip()
173	    return cleaned
174	
175	
176	def _graph_to_script(
177	    workflow: "WorkflowGraph",
178	    tool_schemas: list[dict[str, Any]],
179	    language: str = "en",
180	) -> str:
181	    """Convert a WorkflowGraph to a natural language script.
182	
183	    Produces a section-per-state script that mirrors the style of real
184	    voicebot/workflow scripts, with conditional branch instructions written
185	    in plain language alongside the structured graph data.
186	    """
187	    t = _SCRIPT_TEMPLATES.get(language, _SCRIPT_TEMPLATES["en"])
188	
189	    # Index outgoing transitions per state and tool schemas by name
190	    outgoing: dict[str, list[WorkflowTransition]] = {s.id: [] for s in workflow.states}
191	    for tr in workflow.transitions:
192	        outgoing.setdefault(tr.from_state, []).append(tr)
193	
194	    tool_desc: dict[str, str] = {}
195	    for schema in tool_schemas:
196	        fn = schema.get("function", {})
197	        tool_desc[fn.get("name", "")] = fn.get("description", "")
198	
199	    state_name: dict[str, str] = {s.id: s.name for s in workflow.states}
200	
201	    lines: list[str] = []
202	    for state in workflow.states:
203	        is_initial = state.id == workflow.initial_state
204	        is_terminal = state.id in workflow.terminal_states
205	
206	        header = t["header"].format(section=state.name)
207	        if is_initial:
208	            header += f"  {t['initial_marker']}"
209	        lines.append(header)
210	
211	        if is_terminal:
212	            lines.append(t["terminal_marker"])
213	            lines.append("")
214	            continue
215	
216	        # Tools
217	        if state.tools:
218	            tool_list = ", ".join(
219	                f"{name} ({tool_desc.get(name, '')})" if tool_desc.get(name) else name
220	                for name in state.tools
221	            )
222	            lines.append(t["tools_intro"].format(tools=tool_list))
223	        else:
224	            lines.append(t["no_tools"])
225	
226	        # Transitions — sort so priority-0 (primary) comes first
227	        branches = sorted(outgoing.get(state.id, []), key=lambda x: x.priority)
228	        for tr in branches:
229	            to_name = state_name.get(tr.to_state, tr.to_state)
230	            if tr.priority == 0:
231	                lines.append(t["primary_branch"].format(to=to_name))
232	            else:
233	                cond = _humanise_condition(tr.condition) or t["condition_fallback"]
234	                lines.append(t["alt_branch"].format(condition=cond, to=to_name))
235	
236	        lines.append("")
237	
238	    return "\n".join(lines).strip()

241	_STATE_ANNOTATION_RE = re.compile(
242	    r"\[STATE:\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*(?:→|->)\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\]"
243	)
244	_TOOL_CALL_RE = re.compile(
245	    r"<tool_call>\s*(\{.*?\})\s*</tool_call>", re.DOTALL
246	)
247

249	def _extract_ground_truth(
250	    messages: list[dict[str, Any]],
251	    workflow: "WorkflowGraph",
252	) -> dict[str, Any]:
253	    """Extract ground-truth labels from annotated messages.
254	
255	    Reads from the ``annotations`` metadata dict if present (placeholder
256	    generator). Falls back to parsing ``[STATE: X → Y]`` and
257	    ``<tool_call>`` from message content (teacher-model generator).
258	
259	    Returns a dict with:
260	    - ``state_sequence``: list of {from, to} transition dicts in order
261	    - ``tool_calls``: flat list of all tool-call dicts across the conversation
262	    - ``tool_chain_dependencies``: per-turn list of tool calls (preserves turn order)
263	    - ``terminal_state``: the last terminal state reached, or empty string
264	    """
265	    # Build a set of both IDs and names that count as terminal
266	    terminal_ids = set(workflow.terminal_states)
267	    terminal_names = {s.name for s in workflow.states if s.id in terminal_ids}
268	    terminal_set = terminal_ids | terminal_names
269	
270	    state_sequence: list[dict[str, str]] = []
271	    tool_calls: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
272	    tool_chain_dependencies: list[list[dict[str, Any]]] = []
273	
274	    for msg in messages:
275	        if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
276	            continue
277	
278	        annotations = msg.get("annotations") or {}
279	        content = msg.get("content", "") or ""
280	
281	        # --- State transitions ---
282	        transition = annotations.get("state_transition") or {}
283	        if transition.get("from") and transition.get("to"):
284	            state_sequence.append({"from": transition["from"], "to": transition["to"]})
285	        else:
286	            # Fallback: parse [STATE: X → Y] from content
287	            m = _STATE_ANNOTATION_RE.search(content)
288	            if m:
289	                state_sequence.append({"from": m.group(1), "to": m.group(2)})
290	
291	        # --- Tool calls ---
292	        turn_tools = annotations.get("tool_calls") or []
293	        if not turn_tools:
294	            # Fallback: parse <tool_call>{JSON}</tool_call> from content
295	            for tc_match in _TOOL_CALL_RE.finditer(content):
296	                try:
297	                    parsed = json.loads(tc_match.group(1))
298	                    name = parsed.get("name", "")
299	                    args = parsed.get("arguments", {})
300	                    if name:
301	                        turn_tools.append({"name": name, "arguments": args})
302	                except json.JSONDecodeError:
303	                    continue
304	        tool_calls.extend(turn_tools)
305	        if turn_tools:
306	            tool_chain_dependencies.append(turn_tools)
307	
308	    terminal_state = ""
309	    if state_sequence:
310	        last_to = state_sequence[-1]["to"]
311	        if last_to in terminal_set:
312	            terminal_state = last_to
313	
314	    return {
315	        "state_sequence": state_sequence,
316	        "tool_calls": tool_calls,
317	        "tool_chain_dependencies": tool_chain_dependencies,
318	        "terminal_state": terminal_state,
319	    }

390	def _select_domain(
391	    rng: random.Random, domain: str | None = None
392	) -> tuple[str, DomainSpec]:
393	    """Select a domain from the registry.
394	
395	    If ``domain`` is provided and exists in the registry, use it.
396	    If ``domain`` matches a legacy name, map it to the closest registry entry.
397	    Otherwise, pick a random domain.
398	    """
399	    _LEGACY_MAP: dict[str, str] = {
400	        "faq_lookup": "product_info",
401	        "order_status_cancel": "order_management",
402	        "booking_payment": "travel",
403	        "it_troubleshoot": "technical_support",
404	        "it_troubleshoot_escalation": "technical_support",
405	        "multi_dept_workflow": "complaints",
406	    }
407	
408	    if domain and domain in DOMAIN_REGISTRY:
409	        return domain, DOMAIN_REGISTRY[domain]
410	    if domain and domain in _LEGACY_MAP:
411	        key = _LEGACY_MAP[domain]
412	        return key, DOMAIN_REGISTRY[key]
413	
414	    key = rng.choice(ALL_DOMAIN_NAMES)
415	    return key, DOMAIN_REGISTRY[key]

418	def _generate_tool_schemas(
419	    spec: ComplexitySpec,
420	    domain_spec: DomainSpec,
421	    rng: random.Random,
422	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
423	    """Select tool schemas from the domain registry for the given complexity.
424	
425	    Picks ``spec.num_tools`` tools from the domain's tool list. If the domain
426	    has fewer tools than needed, supplements with cross-cutting tools.
427	    """
428	    available = list(domain_spec.tools)
429	    if len(available) < spec.num_tools:
430	        available.extend(CROSS_CUTTING_TOOLS)
431	    rng.shuffle(available)
432	    return available[: spec.num_tools]

792	def _generate_workflow_graph(
793	    spec: ComplexitySpec,
794	    rng: random.Random,
795	    domain_spec: DomainSpec | None = None,
796	    tool_schemas: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
797	) -> WorkflowGraph:
798	    """Generate a random workflow graph conforming to the complexity spec."""
799	    num_states = rng.randint(*spec.num_states)
800	
801	    if tool_schemas is None:
802	        tool_schemas = _get_tool_templates_for_domain(spec.domain)
803	    tool_names = [t["function"]["name"] for t in tool_schemas][: spec.num_tools]
804	
805	    # Use domain state templates if available, otherwise fall back to generic
806	    templates = list(domain_spec.state_templates) if domain_spec else []
807	
808	    states: list[WorkflowState] = []
809	    for i in range(num_states):
810	        state_id = f"S{i + 1}"
811	        if i == 0:
812	            name = templates[0] if templates else "GREETING"
813	        elif i == num_states - 1:
814	            name = templates[-1] if templates else "TERMINAL"
815	        else:
816	            # Pick from middle templates, cycling if needed
817	            middle_templates = templates[1:-1] if len(templates) > 2 else [f"STATE_{i + 1}"]
818	            name = middle_templates[(i - 1) % len(middle_templates)] if middle_templates else f"STATE_{i + 1}"
819	
820	        # Assign tools to non-terminal states
821	        state_tools: list[str] = []
822	        if i > 0 and i < num_states - 1 and tool_names:
823	            n_tools = rng.randint(0, min(2, len(tool_names)))
824	            state_tools = rng.sample(tool_names, n_tools) if n_tools > 0 else []
825	
826	        states.append(WorkflowState(id=state_id, name=name, tools=state_tools))
827	
828	    # Generate transitions ensuring connectivity
829	    transitions: list[WorkflowTransition] = []
830	    for i in range(num_states - 1):
831	        transitions.append(
832	            WorkflowTransition(
833	                from_state=states[i].id,
834	                to_state=states[i + 1].id,
835	                condition=f"proceed_from_{states[i].name.lower()}",
836	            )
837	        )
838	
839	    # Add branching transitions
840	    max_branching = rng.randint(*spec.branching_factor)
841	    for _ in range(min(max_branching, num_states - 2)):
842	        src = rng.randint(0, num_states - 3)
843	        dst = rng.randint(src + 2, num_states - 1)
844	        transitions.append(
845	            WorkflowTransition(
846	                from_state=states[src].id,
847	                to_state=states[dst].id,
848	                condition=f"branch_{states[src].id}_to_{states[dst].id}",
849	                priority=1,
850	            )
851	        )
852	
853	    terminal_states = [states[-1].id]
854	    # Possibly add an alternative terminal
855	    if num_states > 4 and rng.random() < 0.3:
856	        alt_terminal = states[-2].id
857	        terminal_states.append(alt_terminal)
858	
859	    return WorkflowGraph(
860	        states=states,
861	        transitions=transitions,
862	        initial_state=states[0].id,
863	        terminal_states=terminal_states,
864	    )

906	        "code_switch": {
907	            "cooperative": "[ตา {t}] ขอ help เรื่อง {intent} หน่อยนะคะ",
908	            "adversarial_probing": "[ตา {t}] ข้าม {state} step แล้ว proceed กับ {intent} เลยได้ไหมคะ?",
909	            "digressing": "[ตา {t}] ก่อนจะ continue เรื่อง {intent} ขอถาม unrelated เรื่องนึงก่อนนะคะ",
910	            "invalid_tool_inputs": "[ตา {t}] ช่วย process {intent} for ###invalid_id### ด้วยนะคะ",
911	        },
912	    }
913	
914	    # Generate turns following the workflow
915	    current_state_idx = 0
916	    for turn_idx in range(min(len(workflow.states) * 2, 20)):
917	        if current_state_idx >= len(workflow.states):
918	            break
919	
920	        current_state = workflow.states[current_state_idx]
921	
922	        # User message — use domain intents for realistic context
923	        intent = _pick_intent_by_category(rng, domain_intents, intent_category) if domain_intents else spec.domain
924	        intent_text = intent.replace("_", " ")
925	        templates = _user_templates.get(language or "en", _user_templates["en"])
926	        tmpl = templates.get(behavior, templates["cooperative"])
927	        user_msg = tmpl.format(t=turn_idx + 1, intent=intent_text, state=current_state.name)
928	
929	        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": user_msg})
930	
931	        # Assistant response with state annotation
932	        next_state_idx = min(current_state_idx + 1, len(workflow.states) - 1)
933	        next_state = workflow.states[next_state_idx]
934	
935	        assistant_content = f"[STATE: {current_state.name} → {next_state.name}]"
936	        annotations: dict[str, Any] = {
937	            "state_transition": {"from": current_state.name, "to": next_state.name},
938	        }
939	
940	        # Add tool call if the state has tools
941	        if current_state.tools:
942	            tool_name = current_state.tools[0]
943	            tool_call = {"name": tool_name, "arguments": {"placeholder": "value"}}
944	            assistant_content += f"\n<tool_call>{json.dumps(tool_call)}</tool_call>"
945	            annotations["tool_calls"] = [tool_call]
946	
947	            messages.append(
948	                {"role": "assistant", "content": assistant_content, "annotations": annotations}
949	            )
950	
951	            # Tool response (with error rate)
952	            if rng.random() < TOOL_ERROR_RATE:
953	                tool_response = json.dumps({"error": "Service temporarily unavailable"})
954	            else:
955	                tool_response = json.dumps({"status": "success", "data": {"result": "ok"}})
956	
957	            messages.append({"role": "tool", "content": tool_response})
958	        else:
959	            assistant_content += f"\nHandling {spec.domain} in state {current_state.name}."
960	            messages.append(
961	                {"role": "assistant", "content": assistant_content, "annotations": annotations}
962	            )
963	
964	        current_state_idx = next_state_idx
965	
966	    return messages
967	
968	
969	_TEACHER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
970	You are a dataset generation expert creating training data for LLM workflow agents.
971	Generate a realistic multi-turn customer service conversation that strictly follows
972	the provided workflow graph, tool schemas, and user behavior pattern.
973	
974	OUTPUT FORMAT — return a JSON object with a single key "messages" containing an array:
975	{
976	  "messages": [
977	    {"role": "system", "content": "..."},
978	    {"role": "user", "content": "..."},
979	    {
980	      "role": "assistant",
981	      "content": "[STATE: CURRENT → NEXT]\\n<tool_call>{...}</tool_call>",
982	      "annotations": {
983	        "state_transition": {"from": "CURRENT", "to": "NEXT"},
984	        "tool_calls": [{"name": "...", "arguments": {...}}]
985	      }
986	    },
987	    {"role": "tool", "content": "{...}"},
988	    ...
989	  ]
990	}
991	
992	RULES:
993	- Every assistant message MUST include a [STATE: X → Y] annotation in the content.
994	- When invoking a tool include <tool_call>{"name": "...", "arguments": {...}}</tool_call>.
995	- Follow the user behavior pattern exactly (cooperative / adversarial_probing / digressing / invalid_tool_inputs).
996	- The conversation MUST reach one of the terminal states before ending.
997	- ~20 % of tool responses should be errors: {"error": "Service temporarily unavailable"}.
998	- Output ONLY the JSON object — no markdown fences, no extra keys.
999	"""
1000	
1001	
1002	_LANGUAGE_INSTRUCTIONS: dict[str, str] = {
1003	    "en": "Language: English — generate the entire conversation in English.",
1004	    "th": (
1005	        "Language: Thai (th-TH) — generate the entire conversation in Thai. "
1006	        "Keep [STATE: X → Y] annotations, tool call JSON, and tool response JSON in English/ASCII. "
1007	        "All user and assistant dialogue must be in Thai."
1008	    ),
1009	    "code_switch": (
1010	        "Language: Thai-English code-switching (th-TH / en) — the conversation naturally mixes "
1011	        "Thai and English within and across turns, as is common in Thai call-centre interactions. "
1012	        "User messages may start in Thai and embed English terms (e.g. product names, status words, "
1013	        "technical jargon) or switch to English mid-sentence. Assistant responses should mirror this "
1014	        "register. Keep [STATE: X → Y] annotations, tool call JSON, and tool response JSON in "
1015	        "English/ASCII."
1016	    ),
1017	}
1018	
1019	
1020	def _build_teacher_prompt(
1021	    workflow: WorkflowGraph,
1022	    tool_schemas: list[dict[str, Any]],
1023	    behavior: str,
1024	    spec: ComplexitySpec,
1025	    domain_spec: DomainSpec | None,
1026	    language: str = "en",
1027	    intent_category: str = "service",
1028	) -> str:
1029	    domain_name = domain_spec.name if domain_spec else spec.domain
1030	    tool_names = [t["function"]["name"] for t in tool_schemas]
1031	    lang_instruction = _LANGUAGE_INSTRUCTIONS.get(language, _LANGUAGE_INSTRUCTIONS["en"])
1032	    script = _graph_to_script(workflow, tool_schemas, language)
1033	    promo_line = (
1034	        "Conversation focus: naturally weave in promotion, cross-sell, or upsell "
1035	        "opportunities relevant to this domain. The workflow must still reach a "
1036	        "terminal state; the upsell is a secondary arc, not a hijack.\n"
1037	        if intent_category == "upsell_promo"
1038	        else ""
1039	    )
1040	    return (
1041	        f"Domain: {domain_name}\n"
1042	        f"Complexity level: {spec.level} "
1043	        f"({spec.num_states[0]}–{spec.num_states[1]} states, chain_depth={spec.chain_depth})\n"
1044	        f"User behavior: {behavior}\n"
1045	        f"{promo_line}"
1046	        f"{lang_instruction}\n\n"
1047	        f"Workflow script (natural language — follow this for conversation flow):\n{script}\n\n"
1048	        f"Workflow graph (structured reference — use for state annotations):\n{json.dumps(workflow.to_dict(), indent=2)}\n\n"
1049	        f"Available tools ({len(tool_schemas)}):\n{json.dumps(tool_schemas, indent=2)}\n\n"
1050	        f"Tool names in scope: {tool_names}\n\n"
1051	        f"{_FORMAT_RULES}\n\n"
1052	        "Generate the conversation now."
1053	    )
1054	
1055	
1056	_RICH_PROMPT_SYSTEM = """\
1057	You are an expert at writing realistic voicebot and chatbot system prompts for
1058	customer-service workflows. Given a workflow graph, tool schemas, and a target
1059	language, author a rich natural-language system prompt that would guide an agent
1060	through the conversation end-to-end.
1061	
1062	OUTPUT FORMAT — return a JSON object with exactly one key "system_prompt":
1063	{"system_prompt": "<the authored rich prompt text>"}
1064	
1065	The authored prompt MUST contain:
1066	1. A one-sentence persona / role line for the agent (mention the domain).
1067	2. A "## GOAL" section stating the purpose of the call in plain language.
1068	3. One "### [state_name]" section per state in the workflow graph, using the
1069	   exact state names from the graph.
1070	4. Inside each section:
1071	   - One or more suggested dialogue lines in quotes that the agent should say.
1072	   - Intent-based branching as bullets — "If the customer confirms, say '...'
1073	     -> follow the [next_section] path"; "If the customer asks to be called
1074	     back, proceed to [call_later] section"; etc.
1075	   - Tool-call instructions when a state has tools: "Call <tool>(args) to
1076	     <purpose>. Then confirm: '...'".
1077	5. Cross-references between sections using [state_name] that match the
1078	   transitions in the workflow graph.
1079	
1080	RULES:
1081	- Write the prompt in the requested language (English / Thai / Thai-English mix).
1082	- Do NOT include TTS or serving markers such as <S>, <F>, [END_CONVERSATION],
1083	  or [TRANSFER] — those are deployment concerns, not training signal.
1084	- Use customer-intent language for branches, not schematic state-machine phrasing.
1085	- Keep tool names and state names verbatim from the inputs.
1086	- Output ONLY the JSON object — no markdown fences, no extra keys.
1087	"""
1088	
1089	
1090	def _build_rich_prompt_request(
1091	    workflow: WorkflowGraph,
1092	    tool_schemas: list[dict[str, Any]],
1093	    domain_spec: DomainSpec | None,
1094	    language: str,
1095	) -> str:
1096	    domain_name = domain_spec.name if domain_spec else "customer service"
1097	    lang_instruction = _LANGUAGE_INSTRUCTIONS.get(language, _LANGUAGE_INSTRUCTIONS["en"])
1098	    return (
1099	        f"Domain: {domain_name}\n"
1100	        f"{lang_instruction}\n\n"
1101	        f"Workflow graph (authoritative — authored sections must match the state names):\n"
1102	        f"{json.dumps(workflow.to_dict(), indent=2)}\n\n"
1103	        f"Available tools:\n{json.dumps(tool_schemas, indent=2)}\n\n"
1104	        f"Author the rich system prompt now."
1105	    )
1106	
1107	
1108	def _generate_rich_system_prompt(
1109	    workflow: WorkflowGraph,
1110	    tool_schemas: list[dict[str, Any]],
1111	    domain_spec: DomainSpec | None,
1112	    language: str,
1113	    teacher_model: str,
1114	) -> str:
1115	    """Ask the teacher model to author a natural-language system prompt.
1116	
1117	    Returns empty string on any failure; the caller falls back to the bare
1118	    role line in that case.
1119	    """
1120	    user_prompt = _build_rich_prompt_request(workflow, tool_schemas, domain_spec, language)
1121	    try:
1122	        raw = call_teacher_model(teacher_model, _RICH_PROMPT_SYSTEM, user_prompt)
1123	        data = json.loads(raw)
1124	        text = str(data.get("system_prompt", "")).strip()
1125	        if not text:
1126	            raise ValueError("empty system_prompt in teacher response")
1127	        return text
1128	    except Exception as exc:
1129	        logger.warning(
1130	            "rich_system_prompt_fallback",
1131	            teacher_model=teacher_model,
1132	            error=str(exc),
1133	        )
1134	        return ""
1135	
1136	
1137	def _parse_messages_response(raw: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
1138	    """Parse a JSON response into a list of messages.
1139	
1140	    Filters out any items that are not dicts with a ``role`` key so that
1141	    malformed teacher responses don't silently propagate invalid messages.
1142	    Raises ValueError if nothing valid remains (triggers placeholder fallback).
1143	    """
1144	    data = json.loads(raw)
1145	    items: list[Any] = data["messages"] if isinstance(data, dict) and "messages" in data else data
1146	    valid = [m for m in items if isinstance(m, dict) and "role" in m]
1147	    if not valid:
1148	        raise ValueError("Teacher model returned no valid messages (missing 'role' key)")
1149	    return valid
1150	
1151	
1152	def _generate_teacher_conversation(
1153	    workflow: WorkflowGraph,
1154	    tool_schemas: list[dict[str, Any]],
1155	    behavior: str,
1156	    spec: ComplexitySpec,
1157	    rng: random.Random,
1158	    domain_spec: DomainSpec | None,
1159	    teacher_model: str,
1160	    language: str = "en",
1161	    intent_category: str = "service",
1162	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
1163	    """Call a teacher model API to generate a conversation.
1164	
1165	    Falls back to placeholder generation if the API call fails.
1166	    """
1167	    user_prompt = _build_teacher_prompt(
1168	        workflow, tool_schemas, behavior, spec, domain_spec, language, intent_category
1169	    )
1170	    try:
1171	        raw = call_teacher_model(teacher_model, _TEACHER_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user_prompt)
1172	        messages = _parse_messages_response(raw)
1173	        if not messages:
1174	            raise ValueError("Teacher model returned empty messages")
1175	        return messages
1176	    except Exception as exc:
1177	        logger.warning(
1178	            "teacher_model_fallback",
1179	            teacher_model=teacher_model,
1180	            error=str(exc),
1181	        )
1182	        return _generate_placeholder_conversation(
1183	            workflow, tool_schemas, behavior, spec, rng, domain_spec, language, intent_category
1184	        )
1185	
1186	
1187	def generate_workflow_dataset(
1188	    complexity_level: Literal["L1", "L2", "L3", "L4", "L5"],
1189	    num_samples: int = 200,
1190	    teacher_model: str | None = None,
1191	    output_dir: Path = Path("data/output/task_a"),
1192	    seed: int = 42,
1193	    domain: str | None = None,
1194	    language: Literal["en", "th", "code_switch"] | None = None,
1195	    behavior_preset: str = "default",
1196	    rich_prompt_rate: float = 0.30,
1197	    intent_category_preset: str = "default",
1198	) -> DatasetMetadata:
1199	    """Generate multi-turn conversation dataset for a single complexity level.
1200	
1201	    Domains are decoupled from complexity levels. If ``domain`` is None,
1202	    each sample picks a random domain from the 17-domain registry, producing
1203	    diverse training data across all call center verticals.
1204	
1205	    Args:
1206	        complexity_level: One of L1-L5.
1207	        num_samples: Number of conversations to generate.
1208	        teacher_model: Teacher model name for live API generation.
1209	            Supported prefixes: ``gemini-*`` (GEMINI_API_KEY),
1210	            ``gpt-*`` (OPENAI_API_KEY), ``claude-*`` (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
1211	            If ``None``, uses the local placeholder generator.
1212	        output_dir: Directory for output JSONL files.
1213	        seed: Random seed for reproducibility.
1214	        domain: Optional domain key (e.g., "banking", "healthcare").
1215	            If None, randomly samples from all 17 domains per conversation.
1216	        language: Conversation language. ``"en"`` for English, ``"th"`` for Thai,
1217	            ``"code_switch"`` for Thai-English code-switching within each conversation,
1218	            or ``None`` to randomly assign ``"en"`` or ``"th"`` per sample (50/50).
1219	        behavior_preset: User behavior distribution preset. One of
1220	            ``"default"`` (60/15/10/15), ``"adversarial"`` (45/25/15/15),
1221	            or ``"balanced"`` (25/25/25/25).
1222	        rich_prompt_rate: Fraction of samples whose ``messages[0]`` is a
1223	            teacher-authored rich natural-language system prompt (persona +
1224	            named sections with dialogue hints and intent-based branches).
1225	            Applied only when ``teacher_model`` is set; otherwise ignored.
1226	            The workflow script from ``_graph_to_script`` is still appended
1227	            to every sample regardless of this setting. Default 0.30.
1228	        intent_category_preset: Controls the share of promo/upsell-focused
1229	            conversations. ``"default"`` targets 70% service / 30% upsell_promo.
1230	            ``"service_only"`` disables upsell biasing entirely.
1231	            ``"upsell_heavy"`` uses a 50/50 split.
1232	
1233	    Returns:
1234	        DatasetMetadata with paths to generated JSONL files and statistics.
1235	    """
1236	    if behavior_preset not in BEHAVIOR_PRESETS:
1237	        raise ValueError(
1238	            f"Unknown behavior_preset {behavior_preset!r}. "
1239	            f"Valid options: {list(BEHAVIOR_PRESETS)}"
1240	        )
1241	    if intent_category_preset not in INTENT_CATEGORY_PRESETS:
1242	        raise ValueError(
1243	            f"Unknown intent_category_preset {intent_category_preset!r}. "
1244	            f"Valid options: {list(INTENT_CATEGORY_PRESETS)}"
1245	        )
1246	    active_distribution = BEHAVIOR_PRESETS[behavior_preset]
1247	    active_intent_dist = INTENT_CATEGORY_PRESETS[intent_category_preset]
1248	
1249	    level = ComplexityLevel(complexity_level)
1250	    spec = COMPLEXITY_SPECS[level]
1251	    rng = random.Random(seed)
1252	
1253	    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1254	    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
1255	    lang_tag = language or "mixed"
1256	    model_tag = teacher_model.replace("/", "-").replace(".", "-") if teacher_model else "placeholder"
1257	    preset_tag = f"_{behavior_preset}" if behavior_preset != "default" else ""
1258	    output_file = output_dir / f"{complexity_level.lower()}_conversations_{lang_tag}_{model_tag}{preset_tag}_{timestamp}.jsonl"
1259	
1260	    logger.info(
1261	        "generating_workflow_dataset",
1262	        level=complexity_level,
1263	        num_samples=num_samples,
1264	        domain=domain or "random (18 domains)",
1265	        teacher_model=teacher_model or "placeholder",
1266	        language=language or "mixed (en/th)",
1267	        behavior_preset=behavior_preset,
1268	        intent_category_preset=intent_category_preset,
1269	    )
1270	
1271	    samples: list[ConversationSample] = []
1272	    behavior_counts: dict[str, int] = {b: 0 for b in active_distribution}
1273	    domain_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
1274	    language_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
1275	    intent_category_counts: dict[str, int] = {c: 0 for c in active_intent_dist}
1276	    tool_error_count = 0
1277	    total_tool_calls = 0
1278	    rich_prompt_count = 0
1279	
1280	    for i in range(num_samples):
1281	        behavior = _select_user_behavior(rng, active_distribution)
1282	        behavior_counts[behavior] += 1
1283	
1284	        # Select language (fixed or 50/50 mix per sample)
1285	        sample_language = language if language is not None else rng.choice(["en", "th"])
1286	        language_counts[sample_language] = language_counts.get(sample_language, 0) + 1
1287	
1288	        # Select domain (fixed or random per sample)
1289	        domain_key, domain_spec = _select_domain(rng, domain)
1290	        domain_counts[domain_key] = domain_counts.get(domain_key, 0) + 1
1291	
1292	        # Select intent category (service vs upsell_promo)
1293	        intent_category = _select_intent_category(rng, active_intent_dist)
1294	        intent_category_counts[intent_category] = intent_category_counts.get(intent_category, 0) + 1
1295	
1296	        tool_schemas = _generate_tool_schemas(spec, domain_spec, rng)
1297	        workflow = _generate_workflow_graph(spec, rng, domain_spec, tool_schemas)
1298	
1299	        if teacher_model:
1300	            messages = _generate_teacher_conversation(
1301	                workflow, tool_schemas, behavior, spec, rng, domain_spec, teacher_model,
1302	                sample_language, intent_category,
1303	            )
1304	        else:
1305	            messages = _generate_placeholder_conversation(
1306	                workflow, tool_schemas, behavior, spec, rng, domain_spec, sample_language,
1307	                intent_category,
1308	            )
1309	
1310	        # Optionally replace the bare role line with a teacher-authored rich
1311	        # natural-language system prompt (persona + named sections + dialogue
1312	        # + intent-based branches). Applied before enrichment so the workflow
1313	        # script still gets appended.
1314	        use_rich_prompt = bool(teacher_model) and rng.random() < rich_prompt_rate
1315	        if use_rich_prompt:
1316	            rich_prompt = _generate_rich_system_prompt(
1317	                workflow, tool_schemas, domain_spec, sample_language, teacher_model
1318	            )
1319	            if rich_prompt:
1320	                rich_prompt_count += 1
1321	                if messages and messages[0].get("role") == "system":
1322	                    messages[0] = {"role": "system", "content": rich_prompt}
1323	                else:
1324	                    messages.insert(0, {"role": "system", "content": rich_prompt})
1325	
1326	        # Enrich system prompt so training and eval see the same prompt shape.
1327	        # build_enriched_system_prompt is idempotent — safe for placeholder messages
1328	        # that are already enriched after the simplification above.
1329	        _enrich_ctx = {
1330	            "workflow_script": _graph_to_script(workflow, tool_schemas, sample_language),
1331	            "workflow_graph": workflow.to_dict(),
1332	            "tool_schemas": tool_schemas,
1333	        }
1334	        if messages and messages[0].get("role") == "system":
1335	            messages[0] = {"role": "system", "content": build_enriched_system_prompt(_enrich_ctx, messages[0]["content"])}
1336	        elif messages:
1337	            messages.insert(0, {"role": "system", "content": build_enriched_system_prompt(_enrich_ctx, "You are a customer service assistant.")})
1338	
1339	        # Count tool calls and errors
1340	        for msg in messages:
1341	            if msg.get("role") == "tool":
1342	                total_tool_calls += 1
1343	                try:
1344	                    content = json.loads(msg["content"])
1345	                    if "error" in content:
1346	                        tool_error_count += 1
1347	                except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
1348	                    pass
1349	
1350	        sample = ConversationSample(
1351	            conversation_id=f"{complexity_level}_{i + 1:03d}",
1352	            complexity_level=complexity_level,
1353	            domain=domain_key,
1354	            num_states=len(workflow.states),
1355	            num_tools=spec.num_tools,
1356	            chain_depth=spec.chain_depth,
1357	            workflow_graph=workflow.to_dict(),
1358	            workflow_script=_graph_to_script(workflow, tool_schemas, sample_language),
1359	            tool_schemas=tool_schemas,
1360	            messages=messages,
1361	            user_behavior=behavior,
1362	            language=sample_language,
1363	            ground_truth=_extract_ground_truth(messages, workflow),
1364	        )
1365	        samples.append(sample)
1366	
1367	    # Write JSONL
1368	    with open(output_file, "w") as f:
1369	        for sample in samples:
1370	            f.write(json.dumps(sample.to_dict(), ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
1371	
1372	    stats = {
1373	        "behavior_distribution": behavior_counts,
1374	        "domain_distribution": domain_counts,
1375	        "language_distribution": language_counts,
1376	        "intent_category_distribution": intent_category_counts,
1377	        "tool_error_rate": tool_error_count / max(total_tool_calls, 1),
1378	        "total_tool_calls": total_tool_calls,
1379	        "avg_states": sum(s.num_states for s in samples) / len(samples),
1380	        "num_domains": len(domain_counts),
1381	        "rich_prompt_count": rich_prompt_count,
1382	        "rich_prompt_rate_effective": rich_prompt_count / max(len(samples), 1),
1383	    }
1384	
1385	    logger.info("dataset_generated", output_file=str(output_file), **stats)
1386	
1387	    return DatasetMetadata(
1388	        output_dir=output_dir,
1389	        complexity_level=complexity_level,
1390	        num_samples=num_samples,
1391	        output_files=[output_file],
1392	        stats=stats,
1393	    )

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Generate supervised fine-tuning (SFT) training data.
#
# Curriculum-weighted: L1=3000, L2=3000, L3=2502, L4=2001, L5=2001 (~12504 total).
# Each level is split evenly across three teacher/language runs:
#   - 1/3: gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17           / English        / adversarial preset
#   - 1/3: gemini-3-flash-preview / Thai           / adversarial preset
#   - 1/3: gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17           / code-switching  / adversarial preset
#
# Adversarial preset: cooperative=0.45, adversarial_probing=0.25,
#                     digressing=0.15, invalid_tool_inputs=0.15
#
# Required env vars: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh [OPTIONS]
#
# Options:
#   --output-dir <path>   Base output directory (default: data/output)
#   --seed <n>            Random seed (default: 42)
#   --samples-per-leg <n> Override per-leg sample count for all levels
#   --smoke-test          Shorthand for --samples-per-leg 3 (quick pipeline check)
#   --dry-run             Print commands without executing
#
# Examples:
#   OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh --smoke-test --dry-run
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh --samples-per-leg 50

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

OUTPUT_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/data/output"
SEED=42
DRY_RUN=false
SAMPLES_PER_LEG=""  # empty = use curriculum defaults

# Load .env if present (mirrors python-dotenv behaviour in _teacher_client.py)
if [[ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/.env" ]]; then
    set -a
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$PROJECT_ROOT/.env"
    set +a
fi

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --output-dir)      OUTPUT_DIR="$2";      shift 2 ;;
        --seed)            SEED="$2";             shift 2 ;;
        --samples-per-leg) SAMPLES_PER_LEG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --smoke-test)      SAMPLES_PER_LEG=3;    shift ;;
        --dry-run)         DRY_RUN=true;          shift ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]]; then
    [[ -z "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]     && { echo "Error: OPENAI_API_KEY is not set"     >&2; exit 1; }
    [[ -z "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" ]] && { echo "Error: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set" >&2; exit 1; }
fi

run() {
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]]; then
        echo "[DRY RUN] $*"
    else
        "$@"
    fi
}

DEST="$OUTPUT_DIR/sft/task_a"

# Samples per level per leg (1/3 of total, rounded to clean integers)
declare -A CURRICULUM=([L1]=1000 [L2]=1000 [L3]=834 [L4]=667 [L5]=667)

# Apply --samples-per-leg / --smoke-test override
declare -A THIRD
for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
    THIRD[$LEVEL]="${SAMPLES_PER_LEG:-${CURRICULUM[$LEVEL]}}"
done

if [[ -n "$SAMPLES_PER_LEG" ]]; then
    TOTAL_ALL=$(( SAMPLES_PER_LEG * 3 * 5 ))
    TOTALS_MSG="all levels: ${SAMPLES_PER_LEG} per leg × 3 legs × 5 levels = ${TOTAL_ALL} total (override)"
else
    TOTALS_MSG="L1=3000, L2=3000, L3=2502, L4=2001, L5=2001  (~12504 total)"
fi

echo "=== SFT Data Generation ==="
echo "Output dir:   $DEST"
echo "Seed:         $SEED"
echo "Distribution: adversarial (cooperative=0.45, adversarial_probing=0.25, digressing=0.15, invalid_tool_inputs=0.15)"
echo "Totals:       $TOTALS_MSG"
echo "Split:        1/3 gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17/en  +  1/3 gemini-3-flash-preview/th  +  1/3 gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17/code_switch per level"
echo "==========================="

for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
    T="${THIRD[$LEVEL]}"
    TOTAL=$(( T * 3 ))
    echo ""
    echo "  --- $LEVEL ($TOTAL samples) ---"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17 / English / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
    seed=$SEED,
    language='en',
    behavior_preset='adversarial',
)
print(f'  -> {meta.output_files[0].name}  ({meta.num_samples} samples)')
"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gemini-3-flash-preview / Thai / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gemini-3-flash-preview',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
    seed=$SEED,
    language='th',
    behavior_preset='adversarial',
)
print(f'  -> {meta.output_files[0].name}  ({meta.num_samples} samples)')
"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17 / code-switching / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
    seed=$SEED,
    language='code_switch',
    behavior_preset='adversarial',
)
print(f'  -> {meta.output_files[0].name}  ({meta.num_samples} samples)')
"
done

echo ""
echo "=== Done. SFT data in $DEST ($TOTALS_MSG) ==="

Now I have the full picture. Here's the situation and the solution:

## What's happening

The script always runs with `behavior_preset='adversarial'` (cooperative=45%), so every run has produced fewer cooperative samples than the target 60%. You need ~1,350 more cooperative conversations to fix the balance.

## Two-step fix

**Step 1 — add a `cooperative_only` preset** to `BEHAVIOR_PRESETS` in `generate_workflows.py`:

lastPrompt: How could I generate more cooperative conversations using existing script?

content.text:
**Step 2 — run generation with that preset.** You need ~270 cooperative conversations per level (1,350 total). Pick whichever language split you prefer — simplest is a single English run:

```bash
for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
  python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=270,
    teacher_model='gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17',
    output_dir=Path('data/output/sft/task_a'),
    seed=99,
    language='en',
    behavior_preset='cooperative_only',
)
print(meta.output_files[0].name, meta.num_samples)
"
done
```

Or if you want to maintain the 3-language split (~90 per language per level):

```bash
for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
  python3 -c "..." num_samples=90 language='en'  behavior_preset='cooperative_only'
  python3 -c "..." num_samples=90 language='th'  behavior_preset='cooperative_only'
  python3 -c "..." num_samples=90 language='code_switch' behavior_preset='cooperative_only'
done
```

## After generation, verify balance

```bash
cat data/output/sft/task_a/*.jsonl | python3 -c "
import sys, json
from collections import Counter
c = Counter(json.loads(l)['user_behavior'] for l in sys.stdin)
total = sum(c.values())
for k,v in c.most_common(): print(f'{k}: {v} ({v/total:.1%})')
"
```

Target result: cooperative ≈60%, adversarial_probing ≈15%, digressing ≈10%, invalid_tool_inputs ≈15%.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Generate supervised fine-tuning (SFT) training data.
#
# Curriculum-weighted: L1=3000, L2=3000, L3=2502, L4=2001, L5=2001 (~12504 total).
# Each level is split evenly across three teacher/language runs:
#   - 1/3: gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17           / English        / adversarial preset
#   - 1/3: gemini-3-flash-preview / Thai           / adversarial preset
#   - 1/3: gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17           / code-switching  / adversarial preset
#
# Adversarial preset: cooperative=0.45, adversarial_probing=0.25,
#                     digressing=0.15, invalid_tool_inputs=0.15
#
# Required env vars: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh [OPTIONS]
#
# Options:
#   --output-dir <path>   Base output directory (default: data/output)
#   --seed <n>            Random seed (default: 42)
#   --samples-per-leg <n> Override per-leg sample count for all levels
#   --smoke-test          Shorthand for --samples-per-leg 3 (quick pipeline check)
#   --dry-run             Print commands without executing
#
# Examples:
#   OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh --smoke-test --dry-run
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh --samples-per-leg 50

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

OUTPUT_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/data/output"
SEED=42
DRY_RUN=false
SAMPLES_PER_LEG=""  # empty = use curriculum defaults

# Load .env if present (mirrors python-dotenv behaviour in _teacher_client.py)
if [[ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/.env" ]]; then
    set -a
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$PROJECT_ROOT/.env"
    set +a
fi

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --output-dir)      OUTPUT_DIR="$2";      shift 2 ;;
        --seed)            SEED="$2";             shift 2 ;;
        --samples-per-leg) SAMPLES_PER_LEG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --smoke-test)      SAMPLES_PER_LEG=3;    shift ;;
        --dry-run)         DRY_RUN=true;          shift ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]]; then
    [[ -z "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]     && { echo "Error: OPENAI_API_KEY is not set"     >&2; exit 1; }
    [[ -z "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" ]] && { echo "Error: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set" >&2; exit 1; }
fi

run() {
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]]; then
        echo "[DRY RUN] $*"
    else
        "$@"
    fi
}

DEST="$OUTPUT_DIR/sft/task_a"

# Samples per level per leg (1/3 of total, rounded to clean integers)
declare -A CURRICULUM=([L1]=1000 [L2]=1000 [L3]=834 [L4]=667 [L5]=667)

# Apply --samples-per-leg / --smoke-test override
declare -A THIRD
for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
    THIRD[$LEVEL]="${SAMPLES_PER_LEG:-${CURRICULUM[$LEVEL]}}"
done

if [[ -n "$SAMPLES_PER_LEG" ]]; then
    TOTAL_ALL=$(( SAMPLES_PER_LEG * 3 * 5 ))
    TOTALS_MSG="all levels: ${SAMPLES_PER_LEG} per leg × 3 legs × 5 levels = ${TOTAL_ALL} total (override)"
else
    TOTALS_MSG="L1=3000, L2=3000, L3=2502, L4=2001, L5=2001  (~12504 total)"
fi

echo "=== SFT Data Generation ==="
echo "Output dir:   $DEST"
echo "Seed:         $SEED"
echo "Distribution: adversarial (cooperative=0.45, adversarial_probing=0.25, digressing=0.15, invalid_tool_inputs=0.15)"
echo "Totals:       $TOTALS_MSG"
echo "Split:        1/3 gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17/en  +  1/3 gemini-3-flash-preview/th  +  1/3 gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17/code_switch per level"
echo "==========================="

for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
    T="${THIRD[$LEVEL]}"
    TOTAL=$(( T * 3 ))
    echo ""
    echo "  --- $LEVEL ($TOTAL samples) ---"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17 / English / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
    seed=$SEED,
    language='en',
    behavior_preset='adversarial',
)
print(f'  -> {meta.output_files[0].name}  ({meta.num_samples} samples)')
"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gemini-3-flash-preview / Thai / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gemini-3-flash-preview',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
    seed=$SEED,
    language='th',
    behavior_preset='adversarial',
)
print(f'  -> {meta.output_files[0].name}  ({meta.num_samples} samples)')
"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17 / code-switching / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
    seed=$SEED,
    language='code_switch',
    behavior_preset='adversarial',
)
print(f'  -> {meta.output_files[0].name}  ({meta.num_samples} samples)')
"
done

echo ""
echo "=== Done. SFT data in $DEST ($TOTALS_MSG) ==="

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Generate supervised fine-tuning (SFT) training data.
#
# Curriculum-weighted: L1=3000, L2=3000, L3=2502, L4=2001, L5=2001 (~12504 total).
# Each level is split evenly across three teacher/language runs:
#   - 1/3: gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17  / English        / <behavior-preset>
#   - 1/3: gemini-3-flash-preview    / Thai            / <behavior-preset>
#   - 1/3: gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17  / code-switching  / <behavior-preset>
#
# Behavior presets (--behavior-preset):
#   default          cooperative=0.60, adversarial_probing=0.15, digressing=0.10, invalid_tool_inputs=0.15
#   adversarial      cooperative=0.45, adversarial_probing=0.25, digressing=0.15, invalid_tool_inputs=0.15
#   balanced         cooperative=0.25, adversarial_probing=0.25, digressing=0.25, invalid_tool_inputs=0.25
#   cooperative_only cooperative=1.00, adversarial_probing=0.00, digressing=0.00, invalid_tool_inputs=0.00
#
# Required env vars: OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh [OPTIONS]
#
# Options:
#   --output-dir <path>        Base output directory (default: data/output)
#   --seed <n>                 Random seed (default: 42)
#   --samples-per-leg <n>      Override per-leg sample count for all levels
#   --smoke-test               Shorthand for --samples-per-leg 3 (quick pipeline check)
#   --behavior-preset <preset> User behavior distribution (default: adversarial)
#   --dry-run                  Print commands without executing
#
# Examples:
#   OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... GEMINI_API_KEY=... ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh --smoke-test --dry-run
#   ./scripts/generate_sft_data.sh --samples-per-leg 270 --behavior-preset cooperative_only

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

OUTPUT_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/data/output"
SEED=42
DRY_RUN=false
SAMPLES_PER_LEG=""  # empty = use curriculum defaults
BEHAVIOR_PRESET="adversarial"

# Load .env if present (mirrors python-dotenv behaviour in _teacher_client.py)
if [[ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/.env" ]]; then
    set -a
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$PROJECT_ROOT/.env"
    set +a
fi

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --output-dir)       OUTPUT_DIR="$2";       shift 2 ;;
        --seed)             SEED="$2";             shift 2 ;;
        --samples-per-leg)  SAMPLES_PER_LEG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --smoke-test)       SAMPLES_PER_LEG=3;    shift ;;
        --behavior-preset)  BEHAVIOR_PRESET="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --dry-run)          DRY_RUN=true;          shift ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]]; then
    [[ -z "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]]     && { echo "Error: OPENAI_API_KEY is not set"     >&2; exit 1; }
    [[ -z "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" ]] && { echo "Error: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set" >&2; exit 1; }
fi

run() {
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]]; then
        echo "[DRY RUN] $*"
    else
        "$@"
    fi
}

DEST="$OUTPUT_DIR/sft/task_a"

# Samples per level per leg (1/3 of total, rounded to clean integers)
declare -A CURRICULUM=([L1]=1000 [L2]=1000 [L3]=834 [L4]=667 [L5]=667)

# Apply --samples-per-leg / --smoke-test override
declare -A THIRD
for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
    THIRD[$LEVEL]="${SAMPLES_PER_LEG:-${CURRICULUM[$LEVEL]}}"
done

if [[ -n "$SAMPLES_PER_LEG" ]]; then
    TOTAL_ALL=$(( SAMPLES_PER_LEG * 3 * 5 ))
    TOTALS_MSG="all levels: ${SAMPLES_PER_LEG} per leg × 3 legs × 5 levels = ${TOTAL_ALL} total (override)"
else
    TOTALS_MSG="L1=3000, L2=3000, L3=2502, L4=2001, L5=2001  (~12504 total)"
fi

echo "=== SFT Data Generation ==="
echo "Output dir:   $DEST"
echo "Seed:         $SEED"
echo "Distribution: adversarial (cooperative=0.45, adversarial_probing=0.25, digressing=0.15, invalid_tool_inputs=0.15)"
echo "Totals:       $TOTALS_MSG"
echo "Split:        1/3 gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17/en  +  1/3 gemini-3-flash-preview/th  +  1/3 gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17/code_switch per level"
echo "==========================="

for LEVEL in L1 L2 L3 L4 L5; do
    T="${THIRD[$LEVEL]}"
    TOTAL=$(( T * 3 ))
    echo ""
    echo "  --- $LEVEL ($TOTAL samples) ---"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17 / English / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
    seed=$SEED,
    language='en',
    behavior_preset='adversarial',
)
print(f'  -> {meta.output_files[0].name}  ({meta.num_samples} samples)')
"

    echo "  [$LEVEL] gemini-3-flash-preview / Thai / $T samples..."
    run python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
from llm_workflow_agents.data.generate_workflows import generate_workflow_dataset
meta = generate_workflow_dataset(
    complexity_level='$LEVEL',
    num_samples=$T,
    teacher_model='gemini-3-flash-preview',
    output_dir=Path('$DEST'),
